Home/Deck resurfacing vs replacement

Deck Resurfacing vs Replacement

Save 40-60% by resurfacing or invest in a full rebuild when it's the smarter choice

Quick Answer:

Resurface ($10k–$25k) if your frame is solid saves 40-60%. Replace ($20k–$50k) if joists are rotting, posts leaning, or deck feels bouncy. Not sure? We offer free inspections we'll tell you honestly which makes sense.

Standard vs. Premium Resurfacing Option

FactorResurfacingFull Replacement
What happensRemove old boards + railings, keep frame, install compositeDemolish everything, pour new footings, build from scratch
Cost (300 sqft)$10,000–$20,000$25,000–$45,000
Cost (500 sqft)$16,000–$25,000$35,000–$55,000
Savings40–60% less than replacement—
Build time1–2 weeks2–4 weeks
RequiresSolid frame (joists, posts, footings)Nothing all new
ResultLooks 100% new on surfaceNew structure + surface
Permit & HOAOften required — we handle permits + HOA approvalAlways required — we handle permits + HOA approval
Warranty5-yr workmanship + 25-yr material5-yr workmanship + 25-yr material
When to chooseFrame is solid, only boards are worn/uglyStructural issues, unsafe, or want new layout

How We Determine: Resurface or Replace?

We offer free deck inspections. During the inspection, we check every structural component:

RESURFACE ✓ (frame passes)

  • Joists: firm, no sag, poke test solid
  • Posts: straight, bases not rotting
  • Footings: stable, not shifting/heaving
  • Ledger: secure, flashing intact
  • Beams: no cracks, no sag

Cost: $10k–$25k | Time: 1–2 weeks | Permits + HOA handled

REPLACE ✗ (frame fails)

  • Joists: soft, saggy, rotting
  • Posts: leaning, base rot
  • Footings: shifted, cracked, heaved
  • Ledger: pulling away from house
  • Deck: bounces when walked on

Cost: $20k–$50k | Time: 2–4 weeks | Permits + HOA handled

Real Examples from Our Projects

Resurfacing$19,500 South Riding

280 sqft deck, 12 years old. Frame solid after inspection. Removed PT boards, installed Trex Enhance + aluminum railings + stair lights. Saved $14,000 vs full rebuild. 1-week build.

Resurfacing$22,000 Brambleton

300 sqft, 10 years old. Frame inspected OK. Trex Transcend + Trex Select railings. HOA approved. 1.5-week build.

Full Replacement$38,000 Leesburg

480 sqft. 18-year-old deck with rotting joists and shifting footings. Full demolition + new footings + new framing + Trex Transcend + Signature railings + lighting. 3-week build.

Full Replacement$28,000 Sterling

320 sqft. Posts rotting at base, deck bounced when walked on. Unsafe replaced everything. Trex Transcend + aluminum railings. 2.5-week build.

FAQ

What is deck resurfacing?

Remove old boards/railings, keep the frame, install new composite. Looks 100% new. Saves 40-60% vs full rebuild.

When to resurface vs replace?

Resurface: frame is solid (joists, posts, footings OK). Replace: structural issues (rot, lean, bounce, shifting).

Cost difference?

Resurface: $10k-$25k. Replace: $20k-$50k. Same size deck the difference is whether the frame stays.

How long?

Resurface: 1-2 weeks. Replace: 2-4 weeks. Permit timeline is the same for both.
